name: cppfront / Cpp2
summary: Herb Sutter's alternative-syntax frontend that transpiles to today's C++; safety becomes the default with explicit `unchecked_*` opt-outs, while remaining 100% interop with existing C++. Personal experiment, not on the standardization track, but used as empirical evidence for the Profiles-style "safe defaults" claim.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Herb Sutter's cppfront is an alternative-syntax frontend that transpiles to today's C++; safety becomes the default with explicit `unchecked_*` opt-outs, while remaining 100% interop with existing C++. Bounds-checked subscripts, null-checked dereferences, mixed-sign comparison rejection, and div-by-zero checks are on by default; `-no-subscript-checks` etc. switch them off per file. Unified `operator=` covers construction/assignment/destruction; `that` parameter for safer copy/move; type-unsafe casts not expressible in Cpp2 syntax.

sections.title: Implementation
sections.content:
Compiles to standard C++20 — drop-in into existing build systems with all existing tooling. The transpilation target choice is deliberate: any existing toolchain that accepts C++20 (debugger, sanitizer, IDE, build system) works unchanged on the generated output, so the safety guarantees are layered without disrupting the existing build/debug pipeline.

Personal experiment (Sutter); not on the standardization track. Cpp2's role in the WG21 safety debate is mostly as evidence: it demonstrates that "safe defaults with opt-out" is workable on a non-trivial codebase without giving up C++ interop, supporting the Profiles position that recompilation-plus-restrictions is a viable safety model.
